summary: The diff addresses all three requirements. REQ-1 is handled by the updated registerOrLogin in backend/main.mo that creates a new profile for unknown principals and returns the existing one otherwise. REQ-2 is addressed by saveCallerUserProfile and updateProfile functions that validate caller identity and persist data. REQ-3 is covered by the updated AuthPage (caches and uses registerOrLogin result to route), ProfilePage (submits and navigates to dashboard), and DashboardPage (loads profile stats and match count). One notable concern is that getMyProfile, getCallerUserProfile, and saveCallerUserProfile still gate access behind AccessControl.hasPermission with a user role, but registerOrLogin no longer assigns that role — instead the useActor hook calls _initializeAccessControlWithSecret for role setup. This is intentional per the architecture but was not requested. Overall the implementation appears complete for all three requirements.

hallucinated_features.description: A prefabricated AccessControl and MixinAuthorization role-based permission system is introduced and applied to profile query and update functions, requiring users to have a user role assigned via _initializeAccessControlWithSecret before they can access their own profiles.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request only asks for validating that the caller principal matches the profile being written. It does not request introducing a full RBAC mixin system with role assignments and permission checks. This adds complexity beyond what was requested and could cause authorization errors if the frontend hook fails to initialize roles.
